Tests the addition of two Number objects in JavaScript to verify the result is 2.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's `Number` object when used in arithmetic operations. It creates two `Number` objects, each initialized with the value `1`, and adds them together. The result of this addition is compared to the primitive number `2` using the strict equality operator (`===`). The assertion will pass if the sum of the two `Number` objects equals `2`, confirming that JavaScript correctly handles the addition of `Number` objects by converting them to their primitive values. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type conversion and arithmetic operations.
